steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence says 3D graphics lack organic realism because they are built from perfect geometric shapes. The second sentence describes what this looks like: unnaturally precise angles and curves. The blank must connect these two sentences as cause $\Rightarrow$ result.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — As such,: signals a logical consequence — the precise angles and curves result directly from the graphics lacking organic realism. That is exactly the relationship in the passage. ✓
  • Choice B — In addition,: signals additional information of the same type, not a consequence. The second sentence is not adding a separate point; it is showing the result of the first. ✗
  • Choice C — In conclusion,: signals a final summary drawn from multiple prior points. There is only one prior sentence, and the second sentence is a consequence, not a conclusion. ✗
  • Choice D — However,: signals a contrast. There is no contrast — unnaturally precise angles are a direct result of building graphics from perfect shapes, not a contradiction of it. ✗
A is correct.
